medical_history.history_of_present_illness: Presented at 6 weeks of age with failure to thrive, intermittent projectile vomiting, and severe constipation. Initial plasma biochemistry showed mild hyponatraemia (Na = 130 mmol/L) and a potassium in the upper normal range (5.4 mmol/L).
medical_history.past_medical_history: Born at 38 weeks’ gestation by elective caesarean section with no neonatal complications. Birth weight was 2616 g (10th centile).
medical_history.social_history: Formula fed, following an unsuccessful trial of breast feeding.

physical_examination.general_examination: Weight was 2710 g (< 3rd centile), with evidence of wasting and reduced subcutaneous fat. Clinically dehydrated with sunken eyes, dry flaky eczematous skin, and moderately severe seborrhoeic dermatitis of the scalp.
physical_examination.specialty_examination: Investigation of a cardiac murmur revealed a physiological pulmonary branch stenosis. Sweat test, and abdominal and cranial ultrasounds were normal. Blood pressure did not reveal any consistent hypertension.

laboratory_examinations.findings: Mild hyponatraemia (Na = 130 mmol/L; NR 134 – 143), potassium in the upper normal range (5.4 mmol/L; NR 3.4 – 5.6), moderate thrombocytosis (platelet count 830 × 10^9; NR150 – 400), increased erythrocyte sedimentation rate (ESR 44 mm/h; NR < 10), marginally increased urea (7.3 mmol/L; NR normal 1.0 – 6.0) and creatinine (49 µ mol/L; NR < 35), all consistent with dehydration.
Initial plasma aldosterone and renin were 8760 pmol/L (NR 300 – 1500) and 4460 mU/L (NR 5 – 100), respectively.
laboratory_examinations.changes_over_time: Aldosterone and renin measured 6 days after commencing treatment were greatly increased at 22,200 pmol/L (NR 300 – 1500) and 14,340 mU/L (NR 5 – 100), respectively.

laboratory_examinations.findings: Urinary sodium was reported as < 10 mmol/L.
laboratory_examinations.changes_over_time: Within a week of treatment, sodium was detected in the urine.

laboratory_examinations.procedure_name: Liver and thyroid function, cortisol, plasma chloride and glucose

other_tests.findings: Revealed a physiological pulmonary branch stenosis.
Did not reveal any consistent hypertension.
The G-banded karyotype showed an apparently balanced paracentric inversion of the long arm of one chromosome 4. The karyotype was considered to be 46,XX,inv (4)(q31.2q35).

The patient’s metaphases were FISHed with panel of six BAC probes spanning the NR3C2 gene (RP11-315D13, RP11635A11, RP11-76G10, RP11-186E11, RP11-655B23, RP11-503L19). The proximal probes were unaffected on either chromosome, but one of the distal probes (RP11-186E11) was split on the inverted chromosome and both probes distal to it were moved to the other inversion breakpoint. This is consistent with disruption of the NR3C2 gene by the proximal breakpoint (4q31.23) of the inversion. The gene is disrupted between exons 7 and 8.

other_tests.procedure_name: Whole genome microarray analysis for copy number variants (CNV)
other_tests.findings: The FISH and microarray studies with the Illumina CytoSNP-12 chip showed no evidence of deletion or duplication at or near the breakpoint, however the array analysis showed a small (151 Kb) duplication at 2q37.1 (2:232057476 – 232208376).

diagnosis.diagnostic_reasoning: The diagnosis was supported by clinical features of mineralocorticoid resistance, including failure to thrive, hyponatremia, dehydration, and increased plasma aldosterone and renin levels. Genetic studies confirmed a structural chromosome disruption of the NR3C2 gene, which is associated with pseudohypoaldosteronism type 1.
